content: Yen-Ling Kuo gave a new talk "Learning Physically and Cognitively Grounded Robotic Assistants" that summarizes our recent work and our vision to build useful and helpful robotic assistants. Watch the video here.
We released the hardware guide and code for our low-cost open-source tactile-force-controlled gripper (TF-Gripper).
We released the datasets, environment, and base models for Moving Out, a benchmark for physically grounded human-AI collaboration.
Yen-Ling Kuo received the NSF CAREER Award for building a computational framework for diverse physical and social interactions with grounding and theory of mind reasoning. Read the news here.
Diff-DAgger is highlighted as an IEEE WiRA Early Career Contribution at ICRA 2025!
